cross_sheet_notes: Single application/hookup page (page 5). Figure 2 is the full ES-FL-2A hookup; Figure 3 detail shows the series connection of multiple E-stop switches into the same S23/S13/S14/S24 inputs. Two WARNING boxes and one IMPORTANT box provide installation cautions (arc suppressor placement across MSC actuators only; never wire a PLC between relay outputs and the master stop control element; check all e-stops individually).

brief.sheet_title: Hookup wiring diagram (page 5) for a Banner ES-FL-2A emergency-stop monitoring safety relay and its two master stop contactors.
brief.purpose: This is the field-wiring diagram for a Banner ES-FL-2A E-stop safety relay. The relay is powered by 24V ac/dc at A1/A2 and monitors a dual-channel emergency-stop button wired into inputs S13/S14 and S23/S24. When every E-stop is released (both channels closed) and the reset button is pressed, the relay energizes its two internal redundant, forced-guided safety output contacts — K1A/K2A on channel A (terminals 13/14) and K1B/K2B on channel B (terminals 23/24). Those outputs feed the coils of the two master stop control contactors MSC1 and MSC2 (fed from L1, returning to L2), which are what actually let the machine run. Pressing any E-stop opens both channels, drops out the safety outputs, and de-energizes MSC1 and MSC2 to stop the machine. For fault detection, the normally-closed forced-guided monitor contacts of MSC1 and MSC2 are wired in series with the reset switch in the S33/S34 feedback loop, so the relay will not reset unless both contactors have actually dropped out. K3 (terminals 41/42) is a separate non-safety auxiliary monitor contact.

key_signals.signal: 24V ac/dc control supply to the relay

key_signals.destination: Reset Switch in series with MSC1 & MSC2 NC monitor contacts
MSC1 master stop contactor coil (L1 to L2)
MSC2 master stop contactor coil (L1 to L2)
Auxiliary monitoring (0.5A max, not shown further on this sheet)

brief.troubleshooting_example: If the machine will not start (MSC1/MSC2 will not pull in) after resetting: with power applied and appropriate energized-work practices, first check for closed inputs across the E-stop channels (S13-S14 and S23-S24) — if either channel reads open with the button released, the fault is in the E-stop wiring or the button contact. If both input channels are good but the relay still will not energize the outputs, check the S33/S34 feedback loop: it must be continuous through the reset switch and both MSC1/MSC2 normally-closed monitor contacts. If S33/S34 is open, one of the master-stop contactors did not drop out (welded contact) or the reset circuit is broken — that is a device/feedback fault, not the relay itself. Note: this sheet shows only the relay hookup; it does not document any downstream machine-control logic, so do not assume what the machine controller does beyond MSC1/MSC2 dropping out.
brief.safety_context: The printed supply level is 24V ac/dc at A1/A2; the L1/L2 contactor circuit voltage is NOT stated on this sheet, so treat it as unknown and potentially higher line voltage until verified in the field. For continuity/resistance checks of E-stop and feedback loops, de-energize, lock out, and verify absence of voltage first. For live voltage checks on the L1/L2 / MSC contactor branch, follow energized-work procedures with properly rated equipment. Heed the printed WARNINGS: install arc suppressors only across the MSC actuators (never across the relay output contacts, where a shorted suppressor creates an unsafe condition), and never wire a PLC or other intermediate device between the safety relay outputs and the master stop control elements.
